scope.summary:
Implement embed NodeViews for the four CKC media embed kinds (image, slideshow, album, video), porting the React embed asset resolution logic from app/src/lib/editor/embed_assets.ts and the hsLink NodeView rendering. Each embed kind is a BlockNode in the MT-011 DocModel with kind = EmbedNode, attrs = { embed_kind: String, ref_value: String }, and renders as an interactive egui widget inline in the document.

EXPECTED DIFF SHAPE:
- src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_editor/embeds/mod.rs
- src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_editor/embeds/asset_resolver.rs (async fetch of asset metadata + content URL from the real backend; mirrors embed_assets.ts logic)
- src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_editor/embeds/image_view.rs (single image renderer)
- src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_editor/embeds/slideshow_view.rs (ordered sequence of images with prev/next nav)
- src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_editor/embeds/album_view.rs (grid of images, click to enlarge)
- src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_editor/embeds/video_view.rs (video poster + play button; actual decode via OS media layer or a stub play button linking to the content URL)
- src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_editor/embeds/embed_block_renderer.rs (dispatch from EmbedNode attrs to the correct view)
- Extend src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_editor/renderer/block_renderer.rs to call embed_block_renderer for EmbedNode kind.

ASYNC ASSET RESOLUTION (asset_resolver.rs):
- Mirror the React embed_assets.ts validation pipeline EXACTLY (FAIL-CLOSED):
  - Reject empty ref_value -> EmbedError::EmptyRef
  - Reject absolute filesystem paths (drive letters, leading '/', UNC '\\') -> EmbedError::AbsolutePathRejected
  - Reject '..' or path separator chars in asset_id -> EmbedError::TraversalRejected
  - Reject http/https/file/javascript schemes -> EmbedError::SchemeRejected
  - For album/slideshow: ref_value is comma-separated asset_id list; validate each entry individually
- HTTP call: GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/assets/{asset_id} -> EmbedAssetMetadata
- HTTP call: GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/assets/{asset_id}/content -> raw bytes for image display
- HTTP call: GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/assets/{asset_id}/thumbnail -> thumbnail bytes
- Use reqwest (already in workspace) or the existing handshake_native::backend_client REST client (backend_client.rs); do NOT add a new HTTP crate.
- Resolution is async; use tokio::spawn in the egui event loop's async context or the shell's runtime. Cache resolved assets in a HashMap<String, EmbedResolutionState> keyed by asset_id so repeated renders do not re-fetch.

EMBED STATES (match React EmbedResolution):
- Resolving: show a spinner (egui::Spinner)
- Ok(asset, bytes): render the appropriate view
- Err(EmbedError): show a typed error chip (colored rounded rect with error kind text, never a blank) with author_id = 'embed-error-{asset_id}'

VIEW IMPLEMENTATIONS:
- image_view.rs: decode image bytes with the 'image' crate (PNG/JPEG/WebP/GIF); upload as egui::TextureHandle via ctx.load_texture(); render at aspect-correct width (max = available content width). Click opens full-size overlay.
- slideshow_view.rs: render one image at a time with prev/next arrow buttons; store current_index in view state. AccessKit: author_id = 'slideshow-{asset_id}', prev button = 'slideshow-prev-{asset_id}', next = 'slideshow-next-{asset_id}'.
- album_view.rs: render thumbnails in a wrapping grid (3 per row default). Click on a thumbnail opens a full-size modal (egui::Window). AccessKit: grid container = 'album-{ref_value}', each cell = 'album-cell-{asset_id}'.
- video_view.rs: render thumbnail with a play button overlay. On play click, open the content URL in the OS default player via open::that() (the 'open' crate) OR render a simple progress-bar placeholder with 'Video: {filename}' text. Mark this as a known limitation in code comments; full in-process video decode is deferred.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. Add to handshake_native Cargo.toml (src/frontend/handshake_native/Cargo.toml): image = { version='0.25', features=['png','jpeg','webp','gif'] }, open = '5' (for video OS open).
2. Implement EmbedError enum and EmbedResolutionState in asset_resolver.rs. Write async fn resolve_embed(workspace_id, ref_value, embed_kind) -> EmbedResolution.
3. In embed_block_renderer.rs, match embed_kind to dispatch to the correct view fn. Pass the view state (HashMap per embed node, stored in RichEditorState).
4. In image_view.rs, cache the decoded TextureHandle in view state keyed by asset_id to avoid re-decoding every frame.
5. In block_renderer.rs (from MT-012), add a match arm for NodeKind::Embed that calls embed_block_renderer::render_embed(ui, node, embed_states, workspace_id).
6. Write cargo tests: asset_resolver validates and rejects absolute paths, traversal, empty refs (unit tests, no real HTTP). Integration test with a real backend: resolve a known asset_id and verify EmbedResolutionState::Ok is returned.
7. Assign AccessKit NodeIds and roles to all interactive embed controls as specified above (reuse the accessibility/ registry + snapshot facilities from WP-011).

DEPENDENCIES: MT-011 (EmbedNode kind in DocModel), MT-012 (block_renderer integration point). No dependency on MT-013 (formatting).
acceptance_criteria: An EmbedNode with embed_kind='images' and a valid asset_id renders a decoded image at correct aspect ratio in a kittest screenshot (requires a real backend with a seeded test asset).
An EmbedNode with an empty ref_value renders a typed error chip 'empty_ref' (not blank) in the kittest screenshot.
An EmbedNode with ref_value containing '..' is rejected with EmbedError::TraversalRejected before any HTTP call is made (unit test, no backend required).
An EmbedNode with ref_value starting with 'http://' is rejected with EmbedError::SchemeRejected (unit test).
Slideshow prev/next buttons cycle through 3 images in order; after the last image, next wraps to the first (cargo test with mock resolution states).
Album grid renders 3 thumbnails per row; clicking a thumbnail shows a full-size modal (kittest interaction).
Video embed renders a play button overlay; clicking it calls open::that() with the content URL (unit test with a mock open fn).
AccessKit: 'slideshow-prev-{asset_id}' and 'slideshow-next-{asset_id}' buttons are present in the kittest accessibility tree.
All resolved assets are cached: a second render of the same embed does NOT issue a second HTTP request (unit test with a counted mock fetcher).
cargo test -p handshake-native -- embeds passes with 0 failures.
proof_targets: cargo test -p handshake-native -- embeds outputs 'test result: ok'.

Integration test (requires real Postgres backend): cargo test -p handshake-native --test test_embeds -- embeds::test_real_image_resolve -- --ignored (runs only when backend is available); output shows asset metadata fetched and texture decoded.
kittest screenshot saved to src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/screenshots/mt014_image_embed.png showing a rendered image with correct aspect ratio.
kittest screenshot saved to src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/screenshots/mt014_embed_error.png showing the typed error chip for an invalid ref.
implementation_notes: The 'image' crate decode is CPU-heavy for large images. Decode on a tokio::spawn_blocking thread, then send the decoded RGBA bytes to the egui context via a channel/callback. Never decode on the egui UI thread.
egui TextureHandle must be created on the egui thread (ctx.load_texture); only the RGBA bytes travel across the thread boundary.
Album/slideshow ref_value is a comma-separated list of asset_ids (matching the React parseAssetRefList function in embed_assets.ts). Split on comma, trim whitespace, validate each entry individually.

The 'open' crate is used for video OS-level playback; this is an explicit known limitation documented in video_view.rs. In-process video decode requires a future MT with a media decoder crate (e.g. symphonia + ffmpeg bindings) and is out of scope here.

Asset resolution cache (HashMap<String, EmbedResolutionState>) must be stored in RichEditorState (owned by the shell frame, e.g. via app.rs / backend_client.rs runtime context), not inside the renderer functions, so it persists across frames.
Fail-closed: any EmbedError must render a VISIBLE typed chip. Never render blank space, never panic. This matches the React embed_assets.ts EmbedErrorKind contract.

Backend access goes through the existing WP-011 REST client handshake_native::backend_client (backend_client.rs); do not introduce a new HTTP client. Backend changes are forbidden; consume via API only.

Cross-WP dependency: this MT builds on the WP-011 native shell modules (accessibility/ registry+snapshot for AccessKit ids, backend_client.rs for REST access, app.rs for the shell frame/runtime context). These are delivered by WP-KERNEL-011 and are reuse targets, not depends_on edges within WP-KERNEL-012.

EMBED NODE-SHAPE RECONCILIATION (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-22, MT-011 hsLink lesson - CRITICAL): do NOT assume/invent a NodeKind::Embed block with {embed_kind, ref_value} attrs - MT-011 NodeKind has NO Embed variant, and inventing one repeats the MT-011 wikilink-mark-vs-hsLink-node mistake. The React reference HsLinkView.tsx renders image/video/album/slideshow embeds AND wikilinks through the SAME hsLink node, so the backend almost certainly represents embeds as the EXISTING hsLink node discriminated by refKind (refKind in {wikilink, image, video, album, slideshow, ...}, refValue = asset_id or comma-list). FIRST verify against app/src/lib/tiptap/hs_link_node.ts + components/HsLinkView.tsx + lib/editor/embed_assets.ts (the real refKind values + refValue format). THEN render embeds from MT-011's EXISTING Child::HsLink atom, dispatching on refKind to image/slideshow/album/video views. ONLY if the captured real shape genuinely uses a separate embed node, add THAT exact node to MT-011's model (node.rs + doc_json, add to allowed_paths) anchored to the real shape - never an invented {embed_kind,ref_value} shape.

BACKEND PROOF REALITY (MT-008 pattern + Spec-Realism Gate): AC-1 (image renders) + the real-asset-resolve integration test need a LIVE Handshake-managed backend with a SEEDED asset (GET /workspaces/{id}/assets/{asset_id}+/content+/thumbnail). Prove the asset_resolver binding against the REAL backend if it can be started+seeded; gate the image-CONTENT ACs behind the integration Cargo feature + emit NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF if the asset cannot be seeded - do NOT fake the backend. The FAIL-CLOSED validation (empty/absolute/traversal/scheme rejection) + view logic (slideshow nav+wrap, album grid, typed error chips, resolution caching, decode-failure->error-chip) are FULLY unit-testable STANDALONE with mock resolution states + a counted mock fetcher - those must all pass with no backend.

VIDEO + HBR-QUIET (KERNEL_BUILDER gate): the `open` crate's open::that() launches an external OS app and STEALS FOCUS (HBR-QUIET violation - same reason MT-010 declined it). PREFER the poster/thumbnail + play-button + filename + content-URL-text PLACEHOLDER for video (NO `open` dep, no focus theft, in-process decode out of scope). OS-open via open::that() is OPTIONAL and ONLY on EXPLICIT user click + documented as a reviewed HBR-QUIET exception (user-initiated, never automatic); add the `open` dep ONLY if OS-open is actually implemented. Never auto-open on render.

CRATES + SCREENSHOT (KERNEL_BUILDER gate): the `image` crate (0.25) is ALREADY a runtime dep in the handshake-native Cargo.toml (MT-012 review confirmed) - do NOT re-add or duplicate it (reconcile the pre-existing double-declaration if touched). SCREENSHOTS mt014_image_embed.png + mt014_embed_error.png MUST go to the EXTERNAL ../../../../Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-test/wp-kernel-012-mt-014/ root via external_artifact_dir() + assert_no_local_artifact_dir() - NOT the repo-local tests/screenshots/ the contract names (pipeline fails the MT on git ls-files src/**/*.png). ACCESSKIT VARIANT: field-correct nearest accesskit 0.21.1 Role; ACs assert author_id. Decode off-thread (spawn_blocking, MC-001), album concurrency<=6 (Semaphore, MC-002), traversal '..' substring-anywhere reject (MC-003), decode-fail->Err chip (MC-005).
ports_from_react: app/src/lib/editor/embed_assets.ts (EmbedError kinds, path/scheme validation logic, asset metadata URL pattern, parseAssetRefList for album/slideshow)
app/src/components/HsLinkView.tsx (React NodeView that renders image/video/album/slideshow embeds inline in Tiptap; this MT is the native equivalent)
app/src/lib/editor/embed_assets.ts (EmbedResolverContext.workspaceId -> native: workspace_id passed through RichEditorState)
binds_backend_api: GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/assets/{asset_id} -> EmbedAssetMetadata (asset metadata: mime, size, width, height, original_filename)

risks: Large image decode on the UI thread freezes the egui frame loop for hundreds of milliseconds.
Album grid with 50+ thumbnails issues 50+ concurrent HTTP requests on first render, overwhelming the backend.
A malicious ref_value containing a relative path escaping the asset namespace (e.g. '../../etc/passwd') bypasses the validation check if the check only looks for leading slash.
Video OS open() call succeeds but opens the wrong application if the content URL is a localhost URL that the OS does not associate with a media player.
TextureHandle created from a failed decode (empty bytes) causes egui to panic or show a corrupted texture rect.
minimum_controls: Image decode MUST be on tokio::spawn_blocking; a cargo test verifies the UI thread is not blocked by timing the render call with a 10 MB image.
Album/slideshow must limit concurrent resolution to 6 async tasks at a time using a tokio::Semaphore; a test with 12 asset_ids asserts at most 6 simultaneous in-flight requests.
Validation must reject any ref_value containing '..' as a substring ANYWHERE in the string (not just as a path component start); unit test: '..hidden/secret' is rejected.
Video view must show the content URL as text alongside the play button so the operator can inspect it; the OS open call is gated on explicit user click only.

Decode failure (image::load_from_memory returns Err) must transition to EmbedResolutionState::Err(EmbedError::MediaLoadFailed) and render the error chip; unit test verifies with intentionally corrupt bytes.

research_provenance.source: KERNEL_BUILDER gate + MT-011 hsLink lesson + MT-008 backend pattern + MT-010 open-crate-focus finding
corrections: embeds = existing hsLink node by refKind, NOT an invented NodeKind::Embed; verify HsLinkView.tsx/embed_assets.ts
real-backend asset-resolve proof or NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF; validation+view logic unit-testable standalone
video placeholder-first, no open crate / focus theft unless explicit-click OS-open
image crate already present (no dup); screenshots external; accesskit variant

implementation_result.implemented_by: KERNEL_BUILDER folded pipeline wf_2dd91aeb (impl + adversarial-review + harden)
implementation_result.embed_shape_reconciled: CONFIRMED embeds bind to the EXISTING MT-011 Child::HsLink atom dispatched by refKind (image/slideshow/album/video) - NO invented NodeKind::Embed; the MT-011 model (node.rs/doc_json) was NOT touched. My reconciliation steer held.
implementation_result.bugs_caught_and_fixed: Review caught a Spec-Realism Sub-rule 2 violation the ACs masked: the image content-fetch->off-thread-decode->texture-upload pipeline was built as TEST-ONLY blocks never wired into production (ensure_single fetched METADATA only; the screenshot test manually pre-uploaded the texture - implementer-mock-feeds-implementer-test), so a runtime image embed was a PERMANENT placeholder and AC-1 could not hold even with a live backend. Harden wired the real pipeline: AssetMetadataFetcher::fetch_content + real GET .../content, EmbedRuntime::ensure_image_content spawns the bytes fetch + decode_rgba on spawn_blocking (MC-001) + delivers ColorImage via a ContentDeliveryCell, drain_deliveries uploads the texture (decode-fail -> Err chip), render_resolved_image renders the texture branch + a 'Decoding...' spinner (no permanent placeholder).
implementation_result.proofs: AC-1..10 GREEN (AC-1 headless texture-path proof; AC-7 HBR-QUIET reconciled via injected VideoPlayHandler, NO open crate / no focus theft); fail-closed validation (empty/absolute/traversal-anywhere/scheme, zero-fetch-before-validate) + caching (no second fetch) + concurrency cap 6 + decode-fail->typed chip all unit-proven standalone; 644 lib + all integration green; clippy --all-targets clean; theme + accesskit-id gates green; embed error chip ('Embed failed (empty_ref)') visually verified; screenshots EXTERNAL (git ls-files src/**/*.png empty); image dep feature-unified (no dup), no new HTTP crate, no open crate.
implementation_result.blocked: PT-2 real-image-resolve = NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF: the production ReqwestAssetFetcher binds GET /workspaces/{id}/assets/{id}(+/content) against a real backend, gated #[ignore] + integration feature; run with --features integration -- --ignored + HANDSHAKE_TEST_WORKSPACE_ID/ASSET_ID against a live seeded backend. NOT faked.
implementation_result.carried_forward_MT_069: set_embed_context(workspace_id, tokio runtime handle) into EmbedRuntime has NO production caller in this diff - the shell (HandshakeApp) must call it at E11 host-mount or every embed stays on the headless no-spawn path (permanent spinner). Same host-wiring class as MT-008/MT-010.
implementation_result.carried_forward_MT_016: VERIFY the embed hsLink shape (standalone-block AND inline-with-text) against a CAPTURED real backend content_json fixture (the MT-011 fixture lesson) - the current standalone-block-only handling is a reconciliation ASSUMPTION; inline media hsLinks currently fall through to text rendering. Close when the backend round-trip is proven.
